AN ORDER FOR THE PAYMENT OF MONEY CAN BE CONSIDERED A JUDGMENT FOR COLLECTION AND ENFORCEMENT PURPOSES. (CCP 680.230 AND 680.270). LOOP PROPERLY FILED THIS ACTION TO ADD MS. JUDGE GILLIAM'S TERMINATION ORDER DID NOT CLEARLY EXTINGUISH THE PRIOR FEE ORDERS. THERE IS NO POINT IN STAYING THIS CASE PENDING RESOLUTION OF THE APPEAL BEFORE THE NINTH CIRCUIT BECAUSE THE PROPRIETY OF THE FEE ORDERS IS NOT PART OF THE APPEAL. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge Pro Tem James Fleming address Plaintiff Anna Gatti for her failure to fomerly contest the Courts tentative ruling by 4 p.m. on Friday November 22, 2019. DEFENDANT LOOP AI LABS INC., Motion For Sanctions And For A Protective Order Against Anna Gatti For Failure To Attend And Proceed With Noticed Deposition Under Ccp 2025.430, 2025.420

Defendants Loop AI Labs Inc. ("Loop") and Gianmauro Calafiore ("Calafiore") seek sanctions and a protective order against plaintiff Anna Gatti for failure to appear at the deposition of Calafiore which plaintiff had noticed for San Francisco on October 15, 2019. Said notice of deposition was preceded by two similar notices, both of which were objected to on multiple grounds. The parties had numerous communications concerning the deposition of Calafiore but no agreement was ever reached concerning it.

It is uncontested that Calafiore is a resident of Milan, Italy. Accordingly, each notice of his deposition for San Francisco was beyond the 75 mile radius specified by Code of Civil Procedure Section 2025.250 and no motion under Code of Civil Procedure Section 2025.260 for a deposition at a more distant location was made.

After service of the notice of deposition for October 15, 2019, the parties exchanged objections and communications concerning the deposition without reaching any agreement. The last communication was by plaintiff on September 26, 2019 (Ex. 6 to Decl. of Healy, Ex. 16 to Decl. of Gatti) wherein she "suggest[ed] that you make that date of the third notice of deposition work for the economy of this trial." Neither Calafiore or defendants' counsel ever announced a refusal to appear on October 15, 2019.

On October 15, 2019, Calafiore and two attorneys for Loop and Calafiore appeared for Calafiore's deposition at the appointed location. Plaintiff failed to appear without excuse or substantial justification.

Code of Civil Procedure Section 2025.430 requires the imposition of sanctions where the party giving notice fails to appear for the deposition without substantial justification or other circumstances making the imposition of sanctions unjust. Plaintiff has failed to demonstrate substantial justification for her failure to appear for the deposition on October 15, 2019 or other circumstances making the imposition of sanctions unjust. none of which are present here. Defendants' reasonable attorneys fees and expenses incurred in preparing for and appearing at the deposition scheduled for October 15, 2019 and in making this motion are the sum of $10,711.25. According, plaintiff Gatti is ordered to pay sanctions to defendants Loop and Calafiore in the amount $10,711.25 within thirty days of the date of the order hereon.
The motion is otherwise denied. In particular, the request for a protective order is denied as premature and as not accompanied with a meet and confer declaration as required by Code of Civil Procedure Section 2025.420. Order is signed. Judge Pro Tem: James Fleming; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/JPT)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Or Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Oct-07-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Valeria Calafiore Healy, Esq. of Healy LLC, T:212.810.0377, with Ira Leshin, Esq. of the Law Offices of Ira Leshin, T:415.672.1014, for defendants and moving party;
Janet Brayer, Esq. of the Law Offices of Janet Brayer, T:415.445.9555, for plaintiff and opposing party.


RULING: The tentative ruling was contested and argued. After arguments, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on DEFENDANTS LOOP AI LABS INC., GIANMAURO CALAFIORE's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Or Summary Adjudication, as follows:

Defendants Loop AI Labs, Inc. and Gianmauro Calafiore's motion for summary judgment or alternatively, summary adjudication is denied. Defendants' motion is procedurally and substantively deficient.

Defendants' notice of motion and separate statement of undisputed facts does not comply with California Rule of Court 3.1350. Defendants do not separately state in the notice of motion or in the separate statement what issues or causes of action they seek to adjudicate. (See Cal. R. Ct. 3.1350(b),(h).) Defendants further fail to "plainly and concisely" set forth all material facts they contend are undisputed. (See Code of Civ. Proc., 437c(b)(1).) In addition, most of defendants' citations to supporting evidence are to multi-page exhibits with no pin cites. (UMF 2, 3, 10-19.) The court denies this motion because of the various defects. (See Code Civ. Proc. 437c(b)(1) ["The failure to comply with this requirement of a separate statement may in the court's discretion constitute a sufficient ground for denying the motion"]; Reeves v. Safeway Stores, Inc. (2004) 121 Cal.App.4th 95, 106 [court's discretion to deny summary judgment based on defective separate statement]. In addition, much of the cited evidence is inadmissible or not probative. For example, defendants cannot rely on their own answer to maintain their initial burden of production. (See Hearn Pac. Corp. v. Second Generation Roofing, Inc. (2016) 247 Cal.App.4th 117, 131 [party may not offensively rely on own pleading on summary judgment]; UMF 3-5.) Defendants' citation to Gatti's objections to discovery similarly fails to prove anything. (UMF 14.) Such objections are not "factually devoid" discovery responses. (See Gaggero v. Yura (2003) 108 Cal.App.4th 884, 893 ["a deposition objection and instruction not to answer are not the equivalent of a factually devoid response."].) Moreover, Plaintiff points out that she served supplemental responses to those discovery requests, which Defendants fail to address. (Gatti Decl., Ex. 10.)

Order signed in open court.

Off Calendar Dec-03-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Janet Brayer, Esq. 415-445-9555 specially apearing for Plaintiff.
Ira Leshin, Esq. 415-399-1000 for Defendant Loop A1.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court informs parties the amount sought is excessive. Plaintiff argues and requests an increase. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT ANNA GATTI'S Motion For Reasonable Attorneys Fees And Costs Following Successful Anti-Slapp Motion Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 425.16.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:Cross-defendant Anna Gatti's motion for attorney's fees and costs per CCP 425.16 is granted in small part. Ms. Gatti is awarded fees and costs of $20,688.70, comprised of $20,600 in fees and $88.70 in costs, against cross-complainant Loop A1 Labs Inc. Ms. Gatti is entitled to a fee award for her successful striking of Loop's cross-claim for malicious prosecution. That award is limited to fees that were reasonably incurred. Based on the court's own experience and reviewing all the papers filed on the anti-SLAPP motion and this motion, the hourly rate of $515 for the work performed by counsel for Ms. Gatti is at the high end of the range of a reasonable hourly rate for the counsel who performed the work and thus is accepted by the court. However, the hours claimed by counsel for Ms. Gatti, while undoubtedly spent by counsel, far exceed what is reasonable for either the anti-SLAPP motion or this motion. Based on the court's own experience and reviewing all the papers filed on the anti-SLAPP motion and this motion, the high end of the range of a reasonable number of hours for each motion was 20 hours. The malicious prosecution claim was a classic SLAPP and it took very little effort to point that out. The first prong of the anti-SLAPP analysis was easily satisfied since a malicious prosecution claim always or nearly so arises from protected petitioning activity and that was surely the case here. The second prong was also easily satisfied since the express language of the federal judgment showed that Ms. Gatti had not abandoned her federal court counterclaim and thus as a matter law Loop could not show that the counterclaim had been resolved in its favor. Any reasonable attorney who justifiably claims an hourly rate exceeding $500 should have been able to make these two simple points in far fewer than twenty hours and certainly no more than twenty hours, including both moving and reply papers and attendance at a hearing. This motion too is quite simple because of the statutory entitlement to fees and the uncomplicated procedural and factual circumstances giving rise to the motion. Any reasonable attorney who justifiably claims an hourly rate exceeding $500 should have been able to establish Ms. Gatti's entitlement to fees and present a lodestar analysis in far fewer than twenty hours and certainly no more than twenty hours, including both moving and reply papers and attendance at a hearing. Because both the hourly rate and the hours spent are at the high end of the reasonable range and there are no special circumstances warranting a multiplier, no multiplier is awarded. While some of Loop's arguments in opposition to this motion were clearly frivolous, the court declines to engage in any CCP 128.5 inquiry because to do so only creates further collateral litigation in this already vastly over-litigated case.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-26-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Scott M. Stillman of McGuinn Hillsman & Palefsky, Tel. No. 1-415-421-9292 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. Valeria Healy of Healy LLC, Tel. No. 1-212-810-0377 appearing for the Defendant;
Atty. Ira Leshin of the Law Office of ira Leshin, Tel. No. 1-415-399-1000 appearing for the Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Cross-defendant Anna Gatti's anti-SLAPP motion to strike Loop AI Labs, Inc.'s first cause of action for malicious prosecution in the first amended cross-complaint is granted. The court exercises its discretion per CCP 425.16(f) to hear this motion even though Ms. Gatti filed the motion more than 60 days after service of the original cross-complaint, which alleged a malicious prosecution claim. Ms. Gatti acted diligently in light of the parties' meet and confer discussions. The facts of this case bear no resemblance to Newport Harbor Ventures, LLC v. Morris Cerullo World Evangelism (2018) 4 Cal.5th 637. Addressing the motion will further the purpose of the anti-SLAPP statute to quickly dispose of SLAPP suits. Ms. Gatti demonstrates that the malicious prosecution claim is based on petitioning activity and thus she has satisfied her first prong burden. Loop has not established its second prong burden of showing that its malicious prosecution has minimal merit. "[I]n order to establish a cause of action for malicious prosecution of either a criminal or civil proceeding, a plaintiff must demonstrate 'that the prior action (1) was commenced by or at the direction of the defendant and was pursued to a legal termination in his, plaintiff's, favor; (2) was brought without probable cause; and (3) was initiated with malice.'" (Sheldon Appel Co. v. Albert & Oliker (1989) 47 Cal.3d 863, 871(citations omitted)). Loop cannot satisfy the element of favorable termination. "[T]hat element requires that the termination of the prior action reflect on the merits and tend[ ] to indicate [the former defendant's] innocence of or lack of responsibility for the alleged misconduct. Thus, [a] voluntary dismissal on technical grounds, such as lack of jurisdiction, laches, the statute of limitations or prematurity, does not constitute a favorable termination because it does not reflect on the substantive merits of the underlying claim." (Drummond v. Desmarais (2009) 176 Cal.App.4th 439, 455-456 (internal quotations and citations omitted).) The federal judgment shows that Ms. Gatti's federal counterclaim was dismissed because the federal court declined to exercise supplemental jurisdiction over it. That judgment does not reflect on the merits of the counterclaim. Loop's argument that the counterclaim was abandoned when Ms. Gatti filed an amended answer or answer to the second amended complaint that did not re-plead the counterclaim lacks merit. The abandonment theory is inconsistent with the express language of the federal judgment. Moreover, Ms. Gatti understandably did not re-plead the counterclaim. On October 3, 2015 Loop answered the counterclaim. Ms. Gatti filed the amended answer or answer to second amended complaint on October 7, 2015. Since she was not amending her counterclaim and because Loop already answered it, there was nothing to re-plead. The authorities that characterize a counterclaim as independent from an answer and not waived when not re-pled in a subsequent answer are better reasoned than the authorities relied on by Loop. (Ground Zero Museum Workshop v. Wilson (D. Md. 2011) 813 F.Supp.2d 678, 706.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600 =302/hk

Plaintiff reports matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

SPECIALLY APPEARING DEFENDANT LOOP AI LABS INC., GIANMAURO CALAFIORE'S Motion To Quash Invalid Service Of Process.

DEFENDANTS LOOP AI LABS INC. and GIANMAURO CALAFIORE'S Motion To Quash Invalid Service Of Process is DENIED as follows:

Defendant Loop's motion to quash is DENIED. The service statutes are liberally construed where the defendant receives actual notice. Plaintiff substantially complied with CCP 416.10 as the record indicates that the papers made their way to Calafiore, Loop's CEO who is a person listed under the statute. (See Dill v. Berquist Constr. Co. (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 1426, 1439.) Plaintiff properly substitute served Loop by leaving the papers with the receptionist as he was the person apparently in charge. (See Bein v. Brechtel-Jochim Group, Inc. (1992) 6 Cal.App.4th 1387.

Defendant Calafiore's motion to quash is DENIED. Calafiore received actual notice and the service statutes are liberally construed. Plaintiff's two service attempts on Calafiore demonstrates diligence. (See Espindola v. Nunez (1988) 199 Cal.App.3d 1389, 1392.)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/RBU)
